meta.title: AI Exposure of the Canadian Job Market
meta.primary_rollup_note: The dashboard rolls canonical NOC 2021 unit groups through the 45 official major groups.
meta.metric_model_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables are published at a coarser occupation grain than official NOC 2021 unit groups, so the unit-group labour metrics are allocated using ESDC unit employment weights. Outlook remains direct from the ESDC unit-group release.
meta.exposure_metric_scale: 0-10 display score derived from the share of workers in high-exposure EPIAC occupations
meta.exposure_note: Official EPIAC fields are mapped from published StatCan occupation groups onto the canonical NOC 2021 unit groups.
meta.profile_layer_note: Official OaSIS occupational profiles attach to canonical NOC 2021 unit groups through an explicit generated mapping table.
meta.source: Statistics Canada annual occupation tables rebuilt onto the canonical NOC 2021 spine

oasis.title: Occupational and Skills Information System (OaSIS) - 2025 Version 1.0

family.title: Supervisors in natural resources, agriculture and related production
family.category: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations
family.definition: This major group comprises supervisors in natural resources, agriculture and related production. These occupations come with Training, Education, Experience and Responsibility (TEER) identified as TEER 2. Occupations classified in TEER 2, as those included in this group with a 2 as the second digit of their code, typically require completion of a post-secondary education program of two to three years at community college, institute of technology or CÉGEP; or completion of an apprenticeship training program of two to five years; or supervisory or significant safety responsibilities; or the accumulation of several years of experience in a related occupation found in TEER 3, when applicable (TEER 3 includes occupations with a 3 as a second digit in their code).
family.broad_category_title: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations
family.major_group_title: Supervisors in natural resources, agriculture and related production
family.exposure_rationale: Aggregated from canonical unit groups. EPIAC values roll up unit-level mappings from published StatCan occupation groups.
family.metric_rationale: This major group aggregates canonical unit groups. Labour-market counts and wages reflect the unit-group estimates allocated from published StatCan annual occupation tables using ESDC employment weights.
family.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations (8)

units.title: Supervisors, logging and forestry
units.category: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations
units.broad_category_title: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations
units.major_group_title: Supervisors in natural resources, agriculture and related production
units.sub_major_group_title: Supervisors in natural resources, agriculture and related production
units.definition: Supervisors in logging and forestry supervise and coordinate the activities of workers engaged in logging operations and silvicultural operations. They are employed by logging companies, contractors and government agencies.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations (8).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations (8)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Supervisors in logging and forestry supervise and coordinate the activities of workers engaged in logging operations and silvicultural operations.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Completion of a one- to three-year college program for forestry technologists or technicians may be required.
An industrial first aid certificate may be required.
Several years of experience as a logger, silvicultural worker, or logging machinery operator are usually required.
A chemical application licence may be required.
Formal company training and several months of on-the-job training are provided.

core_competencies.statement: to oversee the use of equipment, tools and materials in logging operations, ensuring resources are utilized efficiently and replaced when necessary
to oversee logging and forestry workers to determine progress and quality of work
to react quickly to indicators of unsafe working conditions and instruct workers on procedures
to monitor large logging sites and assess distant areas for safety hazards or operational efficiency
to ensure records of logging and silvicultural operations are maintained for use in reports
to make informed decisions, ensure environmentally responsible operations and enhance the overall effectiveness of logging and forest management activities

units.minor_group_title: Contractors and supervisors, mining, oil and gas
units.definition: Supervisors in mining and quarrying supervise and coordinate activities of workers engaged in underground and surface mining operations and quarries. They are employed by coal, metal and non-metallic mineral mines and quarries.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Supervisors in mining and quarrying supervise and coordinate activities of workers engaged in underground and surface mining operations and quarries.
employment_requirements: Several years of experience in the occupations supervised are usually required.
Provincial certification as an underground mine supervisor, shift boss, or coal mining supervisor may be required.
Completion of a college or university program in mining technology or engineering may be required for some positions in this group.
Completion of secondary school is required.
additional_information: Mobility between surface mining, underground mining, coal or metal mining sectors may be limited by differences in production technologies and licensing requirements.
There is mobility between employers, especially for supervisors with post-secondary diplomas or degrees.
workplaces_employers: Coal, metal and non-metallic mineral underground mines

example_titles.job_title_text: construction supervisor - underground mining and quarrying
motormen/women and dinkeymen/women foreman/woman (except rail transport)

core_competencies.statement: to effectively schedule and coordinate mining or quarrying activities, optimizing workflow, reducing delays and ensuring projects are completed on schedule
to oversee the use of equipment, tools and materials in mining operations, ensuring resources are utilized efficiently and replaced when necessary
to identify equipment failures, safety hazards or production inefficiencies to organize interventions and solutions
to detect noises indicating equipment malfunctions or safety alarms to maintain safety and operational efficiency
to ensure records of mining and quarrying operations are maintained for use in reports
to understand composition, location and extraction methods of mineral deposits and quarry materials to best plan and direct the activities of workers

units.title: Contractors and supervisors, oil and gas drilling and services
units.category: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations
units.broad_category_title: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations
units.major_group_title: Supervisors in natural resources, agriculture and related production
units.sub_major_group_title: Supervisors in natural resources, agriculture and related production
units.minor_group_title: Contractors and supervisors, mining, oil and gas
units.definition: Contractors and supervisors in oil and gas drilling and services supervise and coordinate the activities in drilling for oil or gas, operating service rigs, or providing oil and gas well services. They are employed by drilling and well service contracting companies and petroleum producing companies. Contractors may be self-employed.

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Contractors and supervisors, oil and gas drilling and services
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Contractors and supervisors, oil and gas drilling and services
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Contractors and supervisors in oil and gas drilling and services supervise and coordinate the activities in drilling for oil or gas, operating service rigs, or providing oil and gas well services.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
Completion of college or petroleum industry approved training courses is required.
Extensive experience in the occupations supervised is required.
A two-year college diploma in petroleum engineering technology may be required.

Certificates in first aid, hydrogen sulphide awareness, blowout prevention, well control, workplace hazardous materials information system (WHMIS), transportation of dangerous goods (TDG) or other safety and technical subjects are required and are obtained by completing short courses.
additional_information: There is little mobility for supervisors from well services to well drilling.
workplaces_employers: Drilling and well service contracting companies

example_titles.job_title_text: oil and natural gas well servicing foreman/woman
platform superintendent - oil and gas drilling
rig manager - oil and gas drilling
well services crew supervisor - oil field services
toolpusher - oil and gas well drilling and offshore drilling
well services crew supervisor - oil and gas drilling
wireline field supervisor - oil field services
directional drilling consultant - oil and gas
well site consultant - oil and gas

core_competencies.competency: Operation Monitoring of Machinery and Equipment
core_competencies.statement: to continuously track operational indicators on drilling and service rig equipment, ensuring machinery functions within safety and performance specifications
to oversee the use of equipment, tools and materials in drilling operations, ensuring resources are utilized efficiently and replaced when necessary
to accurately judge distances when overseeing drilling operations, equipment placement and rig setup
to focus on key signals, safety indicators and operational parameters of drilling while filtering out less relevant information
to oversee complex processes and ensure operational procedures, safety protocols and equipment standards are met
to understand strategies to allocate resources, control costs and plan for worker fluctuations to ensure success and operation of oil and gas drilling projects

units.title: Agricultural service contractors and farm supervisors
units.category: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations
units.broad_category_title: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations
units.major_group_title: Supervisors in natural resources, agriculture and related production
units.sub_major_group_title: Supervisors in natural resources, agriculture and related production
units.minor_group_title: Contractors and supervisors, agriculture, horticulture and related operations and services
units.definition: Agricultural service contractors provide agricultural services such as livestock and poultry breeding, soil preparation, crop planting, crop spraying, cultivating or harvesting. Farm supervisors supervise the work of specialized livestock workers, farm machinery operators, livestock labourers, aquaculture labourers and harvesting labourers. They operate their own business or may be employed by farms and agricultural enterprises.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Agricultural service contractors and farm supervisors
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Agricultural service contractors provide agricultural services such as livestock and poultry breeding, soil preparation, crop planting, crop spraying, cultivating or harvesting.
main_duties: Agricultural service contractors
Provide agricultural livestock services such as artificial insemination, spraying or shearing of livestock or disinfecting pens, barns or poultry houses
Provide agricultural crop services such as plowing, irrigating, cultivating, spraying or harvesting
Assist in the development and implementation of farm safety and bio-security procedures
Hire and train workers
Negotiate the terms of services to be provided
Maintain financial and operational records
May participate in the provision of services or in the operation of machinery and equipment
May operate and repair digital technology and communication tools and equipment, such as robotic milking systems and global positioning systems  (GPS).
employment_requirements: Agricultural service contractors usually require a university degree, college diploma or industry courses in agricultural studies and several years of experience related to the service offered.
Contractors providing certain services, such as artificial insemination and pesticide application, may require training certificates and provincial licensing.
A course or certificate in first aid may be required.

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Agricultural service contractors and farm supervisors
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Farm supervisors supervise the work of specialized livestock workers, farm machinery operators, livestock labourers, aquaculture labourers and harvesting labourers.
main_duties: Farm supervisors
Coordinate, assign and supervise the work of specialized livestock workers, farm machinery operators, livestock labourers and harvesting labourers
Supervise and oversee breeding and other livestock-related programs
Supervise, monitor and enforce procedures to maintain animal and crop health and welfare
Supervise and oversee growing and other crop-related operations
Supervise maintenance of infrastructure, equipment, and supplies
Develop work schedules and establish procedures
Ensure farm safety, food safety and security and bio-security procedures are followed
Maintain quality control and production records
May perform general farm duties
May operate and repair digital technology and communication tools and equipment, such as robotic milking systems and global positioning systems  (GPS).
employment_requirements: Farm supervisors may require a college certificate or other specialized training in agriculture or livestock husbandry.
A course or certificate in first aid may be required.
additional_information: Farm supervisors may specialize in areas such as dairy, poultry, swine, beef, sheep, equine, fruit, vegetable, mixed or other specialty farming.

units.title: Contractors and supervisors, landscaping, grounds maintenance and horticulture services
units.category: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations
units.broad_category_title: Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupations

units.definition: Contractors and supervisors, landscaping, grounds maintenance and horticulture services supervise and coordinate the activities of workers in the following unit groups: Nursery and greenhouse workers (85103) and Landscaping and grounds maintenance labourers (85191). They are employed by landscaping companies, cemeteries, lawn care and tree service companies, nurseries and greenhouses and by landscaping operations of public works departments and private establishments. Contractors may be self-employed.

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Contractors and supervisors, landscaping, grounds maintenance and horticulture services
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Contractors and supervisors, landscaping, grounds maintenance and horticulture services
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Contractors and supervisors, landscaping, grounds maintenance and horticulture services supervise and coordinate the activities of workers in the following unit groups: Nursery and greenhouse workers (85.103) and Landscaping and grounds maintenance labourers (85.191).
employment_requirements: Experience as a landscape or horticulture supervisor or as a landscape or horticulture technician is required.
Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Experience in the type of work supervised is required.
A college diploma, specialized courses or industry-related training and certification in landscaping and horticulture are usually required.
A provincial licence to apply chemical fertilizers, fungicides, herbicides and pesticides may be required.
workplaces_employers: Landscaping operations of public works departments and private establishments

example_titles.job_title_text: public works grounds maintenance labourer supervisor
public works grounds maintenance worker supervisor

core_competencies.statement: to schedule tasks, coordinate work activities and hire workers to ensure project deadlines are met
to oversee the use of equipment, tools and materials in landscaping operations, ensuring resources are utilized efficiently and replaced when necessary
to coordinate the placement of plants, design landscaping features and efficiently organize work areas
to effectively inspect plants, check for pests, complete paperwork and manage detailed maintenance activities
to provide clear direction, resolve conflicts and foster a productive work environment
to understand effective budgeting, resource management and contractor coordination to best manage operations and promote long-term success
